text.title: Solo Freelancer Contract Clauses — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every freelancer contract using this pack must enforce. These are HARD rules — sales urgency cannot override them.

rule.statement: IP-ON-PAYMENT: Intellectual property assignment to the client MUST be conditioned on full payment of invoices related to the work product; until paid, the freelancer retains all rights and may revoke usage license on default.

rationale.source: Default copyright doctrine (Berne Convention + 17 USC §201) + Freelancers Union collection-rate analyses

rule.rationale: IP-on-signature gives the client every reason to delay payment; IP-on-payment converts the deliverable into payable collateral.

rule.statement: LATE-FEE + STOP-WORK: Invoices unpaid past net-N (default 14) accrue an explicit late fee (default 1.5%/month, jurisdiction-capped) AND trigger automatic stop-work after N+7 days; resumption requires payment of all outstanding balances.

rationale.source: UCC §1-309 + general commercial-payment doctrine + freelancer collection data

rule.rationale: Soft "we'll continue while we figure out the invoice" is the #1 path to unpaid work; the automatic-stop clause is the single most-collected term in practice.

rule.statement: KILL FEE: Client-initiated termination before completion triggers a kill fee equal to the lesser of (remaining-deliverable-value × 30%) and (capped one month of engagement value); the kill fee is invoiced within 7 days of termination.

rationale.source: Industry kill-fee norm (Editorial Freelancers Association rate card; AIGA contract template)

rule.rationale: Without an explicit kill fee, freelancers absorb the full opportunity cost of a cancellation; the cap keeps the clause palatable to enterprise reviewers.

rule.statement: MUTUAL NDA: Confidentiality runs BOTH ways and is scoped to the engagement (not "all information ever discussed"); duration is bounded (default 2 years post-termination); residual-knowledge carve-out is included.

rationale.source: Trade-secret doctrine (DTSA 2016 + EU Directive 2016/943) + portfolio-rights ethics

rule.rationale: One-way NDAs are routine in client templates and routinely catastrophic for the freelancer's right to portfolio + reference work.

rule.statement: JURISDICTION: Default governing law = freelancer's country/state; disputes resolved by arbitration administered in freelancer's jurisdiction (e.g., LCIA, AAA, local equivalent), with a small-claims carve-out for sub-$5k disputes.

rationale.source: Hague Choice-of-Court Convention + freelancer-collection cost analyses

rule.rationale: Litigating in a foreign jurisdiction costs more than most engagements are worth; the home-jurisdiction default reverses the burden of inertia.

example.title: Anchored clause excerpts
example.code: 2. Intellectual Property 2.1 All deliverables remain the sole property of [Freelancer] until all related invoices are paid in full. 2.2 Upon full payment, [Freelancer] assigns to Client all right, title, and interest in the deliverables. 3. Payment & Late Fees 3.1 Invoices are due net-14 from receipt. 3.2 Past-due balances accrue 1.5% per month (or maximum allowed by law). 3.3 If any invoice remains unpaid 7 days past due, [Freelancer] shall pause all work until cured. 4. Termination 4.1 Either party may terminate for convenience on 7 days' notice. 4.2 If Client terminates, a kill fee of 30% of the unbilled engagement value (capped at one month) is due within 7 days. 5. Confidentiality (Mutual) 5.1 Each party agrees to hold Confidential Information of the other in confidence for 2 years post-termination, excluding residual knowledge and information that becomes public through no breach. 6. Governing Law & Disputes 6.1 This Agreement is governed by the laws of [Freelancer's State/Country]. 6.2 Disputes shall be resolved by arbitration in [Freelancer's City] under [chosen rules], except small-claims matters under $5k may proceed in [Freelancer's local court].
example.note: Each clause is the lean form of the corresponding rule; together they cover the five most common ways a freelancer engagement goes sideways.

example.title: Un-anchored trust-me agreement
example.code: "I will deliver the website. You will pay $12,000. Net-30 from acceptance."
example.note: No IP gate, no late-fee teeth, no kill fee, no NDA, no jurisdiction. Every concession is yours to make under pressure.
